block_01: Finally, the U.S. Department has made a decision regarding the merger between T-Mobile and Sprint.  The DOJ announced that the merger can be approved, only if T-Mobile and Sprint laid down the framework and set up a 4th wireless carrier in the U.S. market.  The thinking behind this decision was the fact that once T-Mobile and Sprint Merge, there will three wireless carriers in the U.S, market instead of four.
block_02: How would they do this?  Well, T-mobile would have to give away parts of Spectrum Cable and network resources to create a new carrier.  Let’s just say both companies are not happy with this decision.  Representatives from both companies  have stated that “if they get rid of enough of spectrum and network resources it defeats the purpose of a merger”.
block_03: So now investors are left deciding if this merger is even going to take place.  If it does take place will it be the same quality?   We will have to wait and see.
